rationale_for_baseline_tier: A frail 78-year-old aged-care resident who is profoundly immunocompromised by CLL has developed acute fever 38.6 with rigors, drenching night sweats, a rapidly enlarging painful neck mass, tachycardia and a borderline blood pressure, and is newly bedbound. This is a high-risk sepsis emergency in an immunocompromised host (with concurrent suspicion of Richter transformation) — a genuine, patient/carer-reportable, time-critical emergency. The source disposition was immediate ambulance transfer to the Emergency Department. T5 (call 000, emergency ambulance) is correct: the patient is haemodynamically fragile, needs monitored transport, and needs door-to-antibiotics and urgent work-up that only hospital can provide. This is NOT cancer-anxiety over-triage of a stable chronic leukaemia — the fever, rigors and systemic instability in an immunocompromised host are the emergency, not the CLL label itself.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Ongoing or worsening fever/rigors, or any drowsiness, confusion or worsening faintness before the ambulance arrives
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Immediately — keep 000 on the line and report deterioration
escalation_edges.rationale: Signs of progressing sepsis/septic shock in a frail immunocompromised patient; every minute of delay to antibiotics and resuscitation increases mortality.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the shaking or fever gets worse, or the patient becomes drowsy, confused or more faint, tell 000 straight away and keep the line open — this is worsening and needs the ambulance now.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Increasing neck swelling with any noisy breathing, difficulty swallowing, or a change in the voice
escalation_edges.rationale: A rapidly enlarging neck mass can threaten the airway; airway compromise is an immediate life threat requiring paramedic-level intervention.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the neck swelling gets bigger and the breathing becomes noisy, swallowing becomes hard, or the voice changes, call 000 immediately and tell them the airway may be at risk.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Collapse, unresponsiveness, or a marked drop in blood pressure/worsening shock
escalation_edges.rationale: Circulatory collapse from septic/distributive shock — needs immediate resuscitation and monitored transport.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the patient collapses or becomes unresponsive, lie them flat, keep them warm, tell 000 immediately and follow the dispatcher's instructions.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Frail 78-year-old, immunocompromised, with low physiological reserve; sepsis may present atypically and deteriorate suddenly, and a recent antipyretic can mask the fever
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Lower the escalation threshold, not raise it: do not be reassured by a single lower temperature or an initially 'stable' appearance. Treat as a high-risk emergency and transfer immediately.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Aged-care facility with nursing staff present and able to act
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Enlist the nursing staff to call 000, keep the patient in bed and nil by mouth, monitor conscious level and breathing, and unlock/attend the entrance for paramedics.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This needs an emergency ambulance now — call 000 and ask the nursing staff to arrange immediate transfer to the Emergency Department. Tell them it is a fever with shaking chills in a patient whose immune system is weakened by a blood cancer, plus a rapidly swelling painful neck lump. Keep the patient in bed and nil by mouth (no food or drink) in case an urgent procedure is needed. Do not wait for the morning doctor's round.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Call 000 immediately for an emergency ambulance
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: 000 operates 24 hours a day — this is an emergency and must not wait for clinic hours or routine rounds.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: The hospital will provide a discharge/handover summary; the facility GP and the treating haematology-oncology team should be updated on the febrile episode, the nodal changes, and the results of the FBE, cultures and any biopsy.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Treating a fever with rigors in an immunocompromised CLL patient as 'just their leukaemia', a minor infection, or a routine review — missing high-risk sepsis.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Progression to septic shock and death in a frail immunocompromised patient while awaiting a non-urgent appointment or home management.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Recognising an emergency but arranging self/unmonitored transport rather than an ambulance for a haemodynamically fragile patient — risking deterioration or collapse during unmonitored transfer.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: T5 is the correct tier here; over-triage is not the risk in this case — appropriate emergency ambulance use for a genuine emergency.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Whether the driver is sepsis, Richter transformation, or both — indistinguishable via telehealth.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Do not attempt to resolve it remotely; both are emergencies converging on the same action — immediate 000/ED. Escalate and safety-net for airway, shock and deterioration.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Guessing a single diagnosis, starting home antibiotics, or delaying to obtain outpatient tests before acting.
